title: epacris squarrosa
intro: Epacris squarrosa, also known as the rough epacris or rough waxflower, is a flowering plant species in the family Ericaceae, native to eastern Australia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Epacris squarrosa include soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating skin irritations, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or mixing them with carrier oils to create a soothing salve.
